prompt: A black widow spider casting a web net forward. The spider rears its front body upward slightly — the first two pairs of legs lift off the ground while the rear four legs anchor it. The abdomen tilts forward and upward, pointing the spinnerets toward the front. A quick pulsing motion of the abdomen suggests silk being expelled. The front legs then sweep outward in a spreading gesture, as if directing the web in a cone shape. The spider then settles back to its idle stance. Total duration approximately 0.5 seconds.
